option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Log Issues Analysis and Fixes - October 10, 2025
## Issues Identified
### 1. **Excessive DEBUG Log Spam (404 Errors)** **Symptom:** ``` 22:32:05.723 [ValorantChatPoller] DEBUG com.someone.valvoice.APIHandler -- Request https://127.0.0.1:55864/chat/v6/messages failed with status 404 body {"errorCode":"RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND","httpStatus":404,"message":"Invalid URI format"} ``` Repeating every 2 seconds, flooding the logs.
**Root Cause:** - The chat API endpoints (`/chat/v6/messages`, `/chat/v5/messages`, `/chat/v1/session`) return 404 when: 1. Valorant is not running in an active game session 2. The Riot Client is connected but no game is active 3. The chat service is not available yet - Every failed request was logging at DEBUG level, creating excessive noise
**Fix Applied:** - Added consecutive failure tracking in `APIHandler.java` - Only logs first 10 failures, then logs every 50th failure to prevent spam - Logs an INFO message when connection is restored - `ChatListenerService.java` already had backoff logic, which is working correctly
### 2. **TTS Interruption During Shutdown** **Symptom:** ``` 22:32:09.134 [TTS-Worker] WARN com.someone.valvoice.TtsEngine -- Failed to speak text: Hey team from PARTY java.lang.InterruptedException: null ```
**Root Cause:** - Application shutdown was triggered while TTS was actively speaking - The PowerShell process was forcibly destroyed mid-execution - InterruptedException was being logged as a WARNING, making it seem like an error
**Fix Applied:** - Added shutdown check at the start of `execSpeak()` to prevent starting new TTS when shutting down - Changed InterruptedException handling to only log DEBUG (not WARNING) when `running` flag is true - Proper interrupt status restoration with `Thread.currentThread().interrupt()` - Graceful process cleanup with timeout before forcibly destroying
### 3. **Premature Application Shutdown** **Symptom:** ``` Process finished with exit code 130 ``` (Exit code 130 = 128 + 2 = SIGINT = Ctrl+C)
**Root Cause:** - The application was manually terminated by the user (Ctrl+C in console) - This is expected behavior when running from IDE and user wants to stop - However, it happened very quickly after startup, suggesting the user closed it
**No Fix Needed:** This is user-initiated termination, not a bug.
## Summary of Changes
### Files Modified:
#### 1. `TtsEngine.java` - Enhanced `execSpeak()` method with better interruption handling - Added early return check for shutdown state - Improved process cleanup with timeout - Reduced log noise for interrupted TTS during shutdown
#### 2. `APIHandler.java` - Added `consecutiveFailures` counter and `FAILURE_LOG_THRESHOLD` constant - Modified `send()` method to track failures and reduce log spam - Logs restoration message when API becomes available again - Only logs failures initially and periodically after threshold
## Expected Behavior After Fixes
### When Valorant is NOT Running: - First 10 failed API calls will show DEBUG logs - After that, only every 50th failure logs (significantly reduced spam) - ChatListenerService polls with exponential backoff (2s → 30s max) - Application remains responsive and ready to detect when Valorant starts
### When Application Shuts Down: - TTS interruptions won't log as WARNING - Processes clean up gracefully - No scary-looking stack traces in logs
### When Valorant STARTS: - API connection will be detected - Log will show: "API connection restored after X consecutive failures" - Normal chat polling resumes at 2-second intervals - Messages will be properly narrated via TTS
## Additional Notes
### The 404 Errors Are Expected The Riot Client Local API endpoints for chat messages are only available when: 1. Valorant is running 2. Player is in a game (pregame, in-game, or post-game lobby) 3. Chat service is initialized
When just the Riot Client is running (without active Valorant session), these endpoints return 404. This is normal behavior, not an error.
### VB-Audio Virtual Cable Warning ``` WARN ValVoiceController -- VB-Audio Virtual Cable devices missing or incomplete ``` This is **not fixed** in this update because it requires user action: - User needs to install VB-CABLE Virtual Audio Device - Download from: https://vb-audio.com/Cable/ - This routes TTS audio to Valorant's microphone input - Without it, TTS will play through speakers but not in-game voice chat
### XMPP Bridge Status The application is correctly using the embedded `xmpp-node.js` stub since: - `valvoice-xmpp.exe` is not built - Node.js/npm are not available for auto-build - This is working as designed for the fallback mode
## Testing Recommendations
1. **Test with Valorant Running:** - Start Valorant and enter a game - Verify chat messages are detected and narrated - Check that DEBUG logs are at reasonable levels
2. **Test Shutdown:** - Send a test message during TTS playback - Immediately close the application - Verify no WARNING logs for TTS interruption
3. **Test API Recovery:** - Start app while Valorant is not running (see 10 DEBUG logs) - Start Valorant and enter a game - Verify INFO log shows "API connection restored"
## Conclusion
The application is now more production-ready with: - ✅ Reduced log spam when Valorant is not running - ✅ Graceful shutdown without scary error messages - ✅ Better feedback when connection is lost/restored - ✅ Proper resource cleanup
The main "errors" in the logs were actually expected behavior being logged at inappropriate levels. The fixes make the logging more appropriate for production use.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Status Bar UI Improvements
## Changes Made
### 1. Enhanced CSS Styling (`style.css`) - **Increased visibility**: Brightened all status indicator colors - Green (OK): `#00ff88` with glowing effect - Yellow (Warning): `#ffcc00` with glowing effect - Red (Error): `#ff4444` with glowing effect - Blue (Info): `#00d4ff` with glowing effect
- **Better contrast**: - Status bar background: Dark gradient with cyan border - Status labels: Rounded badges with 2px borders - Glowing drop shadows for better visibility
- **Proper alignment**: - Fixed height: 40px for consistent layout - Proper padding: 10px vertical, 20px horizontal - Minimum width for status labels: 80px
### 2. Updated Java Controller (`ValVoiceController.java`) - **Replaced inline styles with CSS classes**: - Removed hardcoded color styles - Added methods to apply proper CSS style classes - Status indicators now use: `status-ok`, `status-warning`, `status-error`, `status-info`
- **New method added**: `updateStatusLabelWithType()` - Allows precise control over status type (ok/warning/error/info) - Properly removes old classes before applying new ones
### 3. FXML Updates (`mainApplication.fxml`) - Added `styleClass="status-label-static"` for field labels (XMPP:, VB-Cable:, etc.) - Added `styleClass="status-label"` for value labels - Ensured consistent styling across all status indicators
## Visual Result The status bar at the bottom now displays: - **Bright, glowing indicators** that are clearly visible - **Green badges** for successful/ready states (VB-Cable detected, XMPP ready, etc.) - **Yellow badges** for warnings (checking, pending states) - **Proper alignment** with consistent spacing and sizing - **Professional appearance** with rounded corners and shadows
## Status Indicators 1. **XMPP**: Shows if XMPP bridge is ready 2. **Mode**: External-exe vs embedded-script 3. **VB-Cable**: Virtual audio cable detection 4. **Audio Routing**: Audio routing status 5. **Self ID**: Current player identity
All indicators now have high contrast and are easily readable at a glance!

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
